calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-03-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mediation, Request For Stay Alternatively A Dismissal Of Proceedings; **First Appearance Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom:
Nyoki Sacramento, Esq. of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad Harris & McSparran LLP, Tel. 415.637.5600, appearing for Plaintiff Ed Van Roden;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT MARGIE ROGERSON'S Motion To Compel Mediation, Request For Stay Alternatively A Dismissal Of Proceedings.

This matter was contested. No appearance by the contesting party.

Ms. Sacramento states that she is not contesting the tentative ruling and confirms for the Court that the defendant was the contesting party. Ms. Sacramento states that their firm received a phone call from defendant's prior counsel, that prior counsel stated that the defendant is willing to comply with what they have requested in the lawsuit, that they have not heard from the defendant's current counsel, and that there may be a settlement forthcoming.

At the conclusion of Ms. Sacramento's comments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's motion to compel mediation is denied.

In Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 536, the court held that courts lack the power to order parties to mediation given the completely voluntary nature of the process. Under CRC 3.853, a "mediator must(2) [r]espect the right of each participant to decide the extent of his or her participation in the mediation, including the right to withdraw from the mediation at any time; and (3) [r]efrain from coercing any party to make a decision or to continue to participate in the mediation." The record is clear that plaintiff no longer wants to mediate. It would be a feckless order to compel plaintiff to mediate since he is free to withdraw at anytime. Given this backdrop, the motion is denied. (But see Breslin v. Breslin (2021) 62 Cal.App.5th 801, 806 [referring to a motion to compel mediation in the context of a probate action with little analysis].)

Lange v. Schilling (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1412, 1416-1417 is distinguishable. There, the court held that if the contract bars recovery of attorney fees by a prevailing party who commences an action without first attempting to resolve the matter through mediation or who refuses to mediate after a request has been made, that provision will be enforced and attorney fees that would otherwise have been available will not be awarded. Lange does not stand for the proposition that the court can compel mediation. An order compelling mediation is antithetical to the entire concept of voluntary mediation.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Bailiff: Park. (301/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mediation, Request For Stay Alternatively A Dismissal Of Proceedings
